description: Reference performance of the original km/kam pipeline from the thesis validation study
source: Thesis validation study, n=10 patients, UMI-based duplex sequencing

cohort.sample_type: Serial blood draws at multiple timepoints
cohort.preprocessing: HUMID UMI deduplication before jellyfish counting

pipeline.km_version: km (Python, RNA-seq tool adapted for liquid biopsy)

notes: SNV sensitivity 77% vs 90-95% for alignment-based pipelines (13-18 pp gap)
INDEL sensitivity 38% overall; near-zero (~3%) for INDELs >7 bp
No FP rate reported (pipeline optimized for specificity in thesis)
False negatives: primarily <0.1% VAF and homopolymer INDEL contexts
Measured on typical 50-target panel with 4 threads
km detect step ~2 min (equivalent to kmerdet detect)
HUMID is single-threaded; main bottleneck at 33% of total time
jellyfish count parallelized to 8 threads in some configurations

known_limitations: SNV sensitivity ceiling at 77% due to fixed walking thresholds
INDEL collapse: k-mer length constraint limits large INDEL detection
VAF threshold ~0.1%: count-2 floor prevents sub-threshold detection
No UMI integration: cannot distinguish PCR artifacts from true variants at k-mer level
Fixed parameters: same count/ratio applied to all targets regardless of coverage
No quality-weighted counting: all k-mers treated equally regardless of base quality
Target design failures are silent: non-unique anchors produce wrong results without warning
